subject: DevOps
title: Small Batches and Frequent Releases
description: A fast breakdown of Small Batches and Frequent Releases for builders.

hook.text: The team saved up six months of changes and deployed them all at once. The release took 48 hours.

buildup.text: Big releases carry big risk. More changes means more things that can break at once.

discovery.text: Small batches reduce risk. Deploy ten lines, and you know exactly what changed if something breaks.

twist.text: Frequent releases also train your process. Deploy daily and the pipeline stays healthy by habit.

climax.text: Amazon deploys every 11.7 seconds on average. Small changes, continuously, thousands of times a day.

punchline.text: Ship small, ship often. Lower risk every time.

quiz.question: Why are small batch deployments less risky?
options: Fewer changes make it easier to identify what broke
Small batches use less server memory
